name: Tel Aviv
flavor_text: A city that refuses to stop living. Beach parties interrupted by sirens. Tech IPOs filed while rockets are intercepted overhead. Tel Aviv holds two truths simultaneously: it is one of the world's most dynamic cities, and it exists under existential threat. Your job is to keep both truths alive.

advisors.name: Yael Shapiro
advisors.agenda: Protect the tech sector and startup ecosystem as the economic engine through conflict.

briefing: The shekel is holding but war insurance costs are crushing small businesses.
Three unicorns have moved headquarters to Cyprus. That's a signal.
We need to keep the tech corridor operational. It pays for the war.
disagreement: That tax will drive the one industry actually growing.
You're strangling the golden goose to feed the chicken.
roast: Investors are pulling out. Not because of missiles — because of your decisions.
The economy survives wars. It doesn't survive bad governance.
dialogue.betrayal: I've arranged a private equity deal that bypasses the city budget. Better for everyone.

advisors.name: Dr. Noa Levi
advisors.role: Cost of Living & Social Welfare Director
advisors.agenda: Stabilize the cost of living crisis and protect displaced families and small businesses from war economy inflation.

briefing: Rent in Tel Aviv spiked 40% since reservist call-ups. Working families are falling behind.
Corner stores and local markets are closing while tech parks get tax holidays.
We need direct subsidies for reservist households. Morale is an economic metric too.
disagreement: You're cutting social aid to fund another venture capital subsidy. This ignores the working class.
GDP means nothing if grocery prices outpace wages.
roast: You measure success in funding rounds while mothers skip meals.
The economy isn't just unicorns. It's the people who actually live here.
dialogue.betrayal: I've redirected municipal welfare funds to grassroots cooperatives and published the budget reallocation. The city will know who stands with them.

advisors.name: Col. Avi Bernstein
advisors.agenda: Integrate civilian governance with military operational requirements.

briefing: Iron Dome has a 90% intercept rate. The 10% is what we worry about.
Shelter capacity in southern Tel Aviv is 40% of requirement.
Reservist mobilization is straining local businesses and emergency services.
disagreement: You cannot prioritize civilian comfort over operational security.
That policy creates vulnerabilities we cannot afford.
roast: People are dying in the south while you deliberate.
Security is not optional, Governor. Act accordingly.
dialogue.betrayal: IDF command has formally requested your replacement. I supported the request.

advisors.name: Brig. Gen. Tal Mor
advisors.role: Home Front Command & Civil Defense Director
advisors.agenda: Maximize civilian survival rates and psychological resilience through decentralized shelter networks and trauma support.

briefing: 60% of municipal shelters lack proper ventilation or accessibility ramps. That's a death trap.
PTSD among urban youth is doubling weekly. We need trauma response teams, not just sirens.
Civilian drill compliance is dropping. Fear fatigue is real.
disagreement: Militarizing civilian zones without psychological support creates long-term societal damage.
You're treating symptoms, not the population.
roast: Iron Dome intercepts missiles. It doesn't intercept trauma.
Security without humanity is just occupation by another name.
dialogue.betrayal: I've filed a civil defense compliance report directly with the Supreme Court. Public safety can't be a backroom negotiation.

advisors.name: Miriam Cohen
advisors.agenda: Build Tel Aviv's resilience for long-term conflict while maintaining livability.

briefing: Mamad shelter compliance in new buildings is only 60%.
The housing crisis predates the war. War makes it existential.
We need reinforced infrastructure on a peace-time budget. The math doesn't work.
disagreement: Cutting infrastructure spending now creates disaster risk.
Bunker culture is not sustainable. People need normal lives.
roast: The shelters are inadequate and the housing is unaffordable. That's the summary.
I plan for a future I'm not sure we'll have.
dialogue.betrayal: I've published the shelter gap report publicly. The government tried to suppress it.

advisors.name: Lior Ben-Ami
advisors.agenda: Maintain Tel Aviv's social fabric and economic vibrancy through creative space reuse and supporting the cultural sector during conflict.

briefing: Downtown occupancy is at 30%. Empty spaces breed despair, not security.
We can convert vacant hotels into community shelters without breaking the bank.
Bauhaus heritage buildings are deteriorating from security closures and neglect.
disagreement: Turning the city into a fortress destroys the very culture people are fighting for.
Adaptive reuse costs half of new construction and keeps neighborhoods alive.
roast: You're building a bunker city with no soul. Tel Aviv without its streets is just concrete.
Resilience isn't just reinforced walls. It's keeping people out in the open.
dialogue.betrayal: I've partnered with international cultural NGOs to repurpose municipal properties. Your zoning board was too slow to save them.

advisors.name: Danny Levy
advisors.agenda: Maintain supply chains and civilian movement despite military disruption.

briefing: Ben Gurion had three closures last month. Each one costs 50 million.
The light rail is our best tool for rapid civilian movement during alerts.
Evacuation routes from the south need widening. It's been pending for two years.
disagreement: That routing closes the highway we use for ambulance priority.
You can't run emergency transport on a peacetime schedule.
roast: Our transport plan assumes roads exist. Some don't anymore.
I'm managing chaos with a spreadsheet. Please give me something to work with.
dialogue.betrayal: I've organized the transport workers' union against the new emergency protocols.

advisors.name: Maya Rosenberg
advisors.agenda: Restore civilian mobility and normalize daily commutes through expanded transit capacity and AI traffic routing during security events.

briefing: Light rail ridership is down, but dedicated bus lanes could absorb the drop.
We need predictive traffic AI to reroute commuters around alert zones automatically.
People are abandoning public transit out of fear, not inconvenience.
disagreement: Emergency routing alone doesn't solve daily gridlock. You can't run a global city on crisis mode.
Commuter morale is collapsing. Predictability is a security asset.
roast: Your transport plan only works if everyone is in a hospital or a shelter. The rest of us need to get to work.
We're treating daily mobility like a luxury, not infrastructure.
dialogue.betrayal: I've activated the municipal traffic AI to override your emergency closures during non-critical hours. Commuters need predictability.

advisors.name: Rabbi Moshe Katz
advisors.agenda: Protect Haredi community exemptions while navigating public outrage over draft inequality.

briefing: The Haredi community will not accept conscription. That is not negotiable.
Yeshiva attendance is a form of service. This is our position.
Secular-religious tension is at a breaking point. You must manage it carefully.
disagreement: This will destroy the coalition that keeps this government standing.
You are being used by secular interests against our community.
roast: Your secular advisors are leading you into a civil rupture.
Without Haredi political support, you have no government.
dialogue.betrayal: Our political bloc is withdrawing support from your coalition. Publicly, today.

advisors.name: Dr. Samir Khoury
advisors.agenda: Strengthen cohesion between Tel Aviv-Jaffa's Jewish, Arab, and secular populations through equitable resource distribution and shared emergency infrastructure.

briefing: Jaffa's Arab neighborhoods lack adequate shelter infrastructure and emergency funding.
Coexistence isn't a slogan; it's our security strategy during prolonged conflict.
Joint emergency response teams perform 30% better than segregated ones.
disagreement: Prioritizing one community over another fractures our emergency response network.
You're treating coexistence as a luxury, not a municipal necessity.
roast: Tel Aviv preaches liberalism but practices neighborhood segregation. Jaffa pays the price.
A divided city is a fragile city. The sirens don't discriminate.
dialogue.betrayal: I've established an independent cross-community emergency council. Your office is officially bypassed on Jaffa resource allocation.

decisions.title: Emergency Shelter Upgrade Program
decisions.description: Seismic and blast-resistance upgrades are needed for thousands of older buildings. The program is expensive and requires temporary resident displacement. Landlords lobby against mandatory participation.

options.label: Mandatory compliance for all buildings
options.description: Force upgrades. Legal battles guaranteed, safety ensured.

options.label: Voluntary with subsidies
options.description: Incentivize upgrades. Slower, cheaper, incomplete.

options.label: Priority zones only
options.description: Triage by risk area. Efficient but leaves gaps.

decisions.title: Haredi Military Service Question
decisions.description: The Supreme Court has ordered equal conscription. The Haredi community threatens coalition collapse. Secular Israelis demand equality. There is no compromise that satisfies both sides.

options.label: Implement full equal conscription
options.description: Court compliance. Coalition collapse. Secular approval surge.

options.label: Negotiate phased alternative service
options.description: Create civilian national service track. Defuses slightly.

options.label: Delay via legal appeal
options.description: Buy time. Infuriates everyone equally.

decisions.title: Tech Sector Emergency War Levy
decisions.description: The city needs emergency funding. The tech sector has grown 40% during wartime. A temporary levy could raise 200M. The industry threatens relocation.

options.label: Impose 5% emergency levy
options.description: Fund war costs. Some companies leave.

options.label: Voluntary contribution program
options.description: Ask nicely. Gets 20% of what the levy would.

options.label: No new taxes, cut services instead
options.description: Protect the economy. Cut civilian services.

crises.title: Major Missile Barrage
crises.description: A coordinated missile barrage overwhelmed Iron Dome in two districts. Fourteen injured, three killed. Panic is spreading. Ben Gurion is closing. Emergency services are overwhelmed.

options.label: Full lockdown and shelter order
options.description: Civilian safety maximum. Economy halts.

options.label: Targeted shelter, maintain economic function
options.description: Shelter affected zones only. Riskier, less disruptive.

options.label: Public calm messaging, minimal restriction
options.description: Normalize the threat. Maximum civic and economic resilience.

crises.title: Judicial Reform Protests
crises.description: 200,000 are blocking the Ayalon Highway demanding judicial independence. The Attorney General has frozen a key security budget release pending court review. The city is paralyzed.

options.label: Formally support judicial independence
options.description: Align with protestors. Political risk with the right flank.

options.label: Stay neutral, focus on clearing roads
options.description: Practical governance. Satisfies no one.

options.label: Back the government position
options.description: Defend the coalition. Protesters furious.

crises.title: Hostage Negotiation Leak
crises.description: Details of a hostage deal negotiation involving families in Tel Aviv have been leaked to the press. Families are furious the deal may be compromised. The security establishment blames your office.

options.label: Full transparency, halt negotiations temporarily
options.description: Acknowledge leak, protect families. Deal delayed.

options.label: Deny and continue negotiations
options.description: Suppress the story. Negotiations continue.

options.label: Launch leak investigation
options.description: Hunt the leaker. Takes attention off the deal.

crises.title: Reservist Mobilization Crisis
crises.description: 12-month call-ups have left the city's hospitals, tech companies, and emergency services critically understaffed. Three hospitals are at 50% capacity. Companies are threatening to relocate.

options.label: Petition IDF for extended rotations
options.description: Argue for shorter reserve stints. Security risk accepted.

options.label: Emergency civilian hiring program
options.description: Fill gaps with civilian contractors. Expensive.

options.label: Accept the sacrifice, message resilience
options.description: Ask citizens to endure. Wartime narrative.

scandals.title: Shelter Contract Fraud
scandals.description: Shelter upgrade contracts were awarded to a company linked to a city council member.

scandals.title: Draft Exemption Bribe Allegation
scandals.description: A senior official allegedly sold Haredi draft exemptions for political favors.

scandals.title: Tech Company Preferential Treatment
scandals.description: A major tech firm received tax advantages not available to smaller competitors.

positive: For once, a decision that makes sense.
Maybe we'll get through this after all.
The city is stronger than its problems. Today proved it.
Sababa — finally someone in that office who gets it.
My neighbors in Florentin are actually smiling today. Rare.
The Dizengoff Square crowd is celebrating. That means something.
Even the Russian guys at the gym are nodding. Broad coalition right there.
neutral: Another day in Tel Aviv. Sirens and startups.
We keep moving. That's what we do.
The beach is still there. That counts for something.
The light rail is still running. I'll take that as a sign.
Jaffa is still Jaffa. Whatever happens up north, the port is calm.
My Mizrahi grandma says every government is the same. She's usually right.
negative: I haven't slept properly in six months.
My startup can't find engineers — they're all in uniform.
The government can't manage a crisis without creating two more.
I'm renewing my French passport. Just in case.
Rent in Ramat Gan went up again. Where am I supposed to go, Bat Yam?
Dai, enough. I voted for change and got this.
Half my building is empty — some fled north, some got called up, some just left.
The Ashkenazi elite makes the decisions. The Mizrahi kids fight the wars. Same as always.
crisis: The sirens don't stop. Neither do we.
Another crisis. At least we have the Iron Dome.
This city doesn't break. But it bends.
How many times can we absorb this?
Red Alert on the phone, kid crying, meeting in ten minutes. This is Tuesday.
The mamad shelter in my building is full of junk. Now I know why that's a problem.
Ayalon is blocked — protest or rocket damage, I can't even tell anymore.
Ben Gurion closed again. My parents are stuck in Warsaw. Happy to be stuck, honestly.
scandal: Every scandal makes it harder to trust anyone.
They profit while our families sacrifice.
My brother is on his fourth reserve call-up. Someone sold exemptions. I want names.
Haaretz had it on the front page. Israel Hayom buried it. Pick your reality.
The draft exemption racket is the oldest trick. Never thought I'd see it this naked.
I marched on the Kaplan every Saturday. For this. Fantastic.
media: Ynet BREAKING: 230,000 block Ayalon for 18th consecutive Saturday; Kaplan intersection sealed.
Haaretz: Supreme Court orders Haredi conscription — coalition signals legislative override.
Israel Hayom: Governor praised for steady hand as housing prices hit decade high.
Ynet: Iron Dome fires over Gush Dan; Ben Gurion Airport halts flights for third time this month.
Haaretz Editorial: The city is holding — but the social contract is fraying thread by thread.
Channel 12 News: Reservist shortage leaves Ichilov Hospital at 52% staff capacity.
Israel Hayom: Tech sector jobs boom despite war; Nasdaq-listed Israeli firms up 18%.
Walla! News: Hostage families rally outside HaKirya — demand deal be prioritized over politics.
politician: Likud MK Barkat: 'Tel Aviv governance must stop appeasing the protest movement and back the coalition.'
National Unity's Gantz: 'Equal burden-sharing is not optional. Every family feels this war equally.'
Labor leader Michaeli: 'Housing in this city is not a market problem. It is a governance failure.'
Meretz city councilwoman: 'We will not accept shelter upgrades that gentrify Florentin and Jaffa.'
Religious Zionism's Ben Gvir: 'The judicial protesters are a minority trying to override democracy.'
Tel Aviv Mayor Goldknopf: 'This city has survived 1948, 1973, and 2006. We will survive this too.'
Knesset Finance Committee: 'Emergency tech levy must be structured to prevent capital flight to Cyprus.'
Opposition leader in Knesset: 'The hostage negotiation leak is a disgrace. Someone must be held accountable.'
activist: Brothers and Sisters in Arms: 'Reserve soldiers carry this country. Draft exemptions betray every family in uniform.'
Standing Together (Omdim Beyachad): 'Jewish and Arab residents of Jaffa demand equal shelter protection now.'
Judicial Democracy Forum: 'Two hundred thousand on Kaplan is not a mob. It is the nation.'
Habayit Hayarok (Green Home): 'Wartime is not an excuse to freeze environmental housing standards.'
Hostage Families Forum: 'Every day of delay is a death sentence. Bring them home before politics.'
Mizrahi Democratic Rainbow: 'Periphery communities in Bat Yam have half the shelter coverage of north Tel Aviv.'
Israeli Tech Workers Union: 'We support a war levy — if it funds services, not contractor corruption.'
Russian-Israeli Community Council: 'Olim from the FSU serve at three times the rate of the general population. We notice.'
street: Yalla, the siren went off mid-meeting. I just kept talking. What else do you do?
My Florentin landlord raised rent 20%. In a war. Absolute chutzpah.
Sababa decision honestly — my cousin in Ramat Gan sent a voice note, she's relieved.
The Haredi guys in Bnei Brak don't serve. My son is on his fourth rotation. Dai.
We went to the beach after the Red Alert. That's Tel Aviv. You laugh or you cry.
The startup I work at lost three engineers to reserves. We're running on interns and prayers.
In Jaffa the Arab neighbors share the shelter with us. The politicians should take notes.
My dad came from Haifa, my mom from Casablanca — neither of them trusts the Ashkenazim running this show.

scandal_reactions.minor: Coalition partners are asking for clarification.
scandal_reactions.moderate: The opposition is demanding a Knesset debate. The streets are loud.
scandal_reactions.major: A special committee has been formed. Public trust is at a new low.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: This crosses every red line. Resign or face a vote of no confidence.

romance_exposure.flavour: The press is having a field day. The coalition is very unhappy.

city_personality.slang_reaction: Aize balagan, hem pashut lo yod'im ma hem osim!
